programme.title: Anita Oday, Göran Engdahl, Roman Dylag & John Poole
programme.desc: Anita O'Day boasts two wonderful concerts from 1963 and 1970 that present the “jazz singer supreme” in impeccable form. O'Day's horn-based approach to singing is in full effect throughout both shows including stand-out renditions in each show of audience favorites “Tea For Two” and “Sweet Georgia Brown” (both reprised from her triumphant appearance at the 1958 Newport Jazz Festival.) This show is a fitting testimony to one of jazz music's true originals and shows unequivocally why she is ranked in the top tier of vocalists along with Ella Fitzgerald, Sarah Vaughan, and Billie Holiday.

programme.title: Lalah Hathaway
programme.desc: Winner of five Grammys in four consecutive years, including the Grammy for best R&B album in 2017, Lalah Hathaway is a force impossible to ignore. The American artist has shared the scene with Prince, Snoop Dogg, Stevie Wonder, Anita Baker, Mary J. Blige, Frank Ocean and many others, an irrefutable proof of her keen musical sense and enormous talent. With a 26-year career, Hathaway is still highly sought after by the biggest names in music. See her transform the 2018 Sing Jazz festival scene into a real R&B club, where she flaunts her voice and stage presence out of the ordinary.

programme.title: Benny Goodman, Scott Hamilton, John Bunch & Phil Flanigan
programme.sub-title: Benny Goodman Septet - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Back in 1982, legendary jazz clarinettist Benny Goodman performed at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague with his septet. The “King of Swing” revisited the atmosphere of the 1930s, when jazz enjoyed tremendous popularity.

programme.title: Kenny Barron
programme.desc: Pianist Kenny Barron's June 27, 2019 appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv was a celebration of sublime jazz virtuosity. The revered elder statesmen of jazz piano brought his regular working trio of Kiyoshi Kitagawa (bass) and Jonathan Blake (drums), augmented by stellar jazzman Marcus Strickland (tenor saxophone) and young lion Riley Mulherkar (trumpet). Their seamless blend of classic bebop and modern explorations spoke of a deep, emotive connection to each note, captivating the Ukrainian audience with lyrical improvisations during this unforgettable concert.

programme.title: Jon Hendricks, Judith Hendricks, Kim Lindsay & Mart Ledford
programme.desc: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Switzerland, Lugano. Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting the understanding of different cultures, tolerance, and co-existence. John Carl Hendricks is one of the instigators of "vocalese", an art form that aims to add lyrics to existing instrumental pieces and replace several instruments with singing. He flaunts this technique at Estival, where he "scat" while playing double bass. Hendricks is known for his mastery of scat, a form of vocal jazz that uses onomatopoeia rather than lyrics

programme.title: Gerry Mulligan, Dave Glenn, Harold Danko & Ed Neumeister
programme.sub-title: Gerry Mulligan Big Band - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. In 1982, American saxophonist Gerry Mulligan gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague. Mulligan was one of the leading arrangers of the cool jazz era, who worked with Miles Davis, Stan Kenton and Claude Thornhill in the 1950s and 1960s. Mulligan's early pianoless 1950s quartet with trumpeter Chet Baker is still regarded as one of the finest groups in cool jazz. Born in New York in 1927, Mulligan spent his life composing, arranging and playing jazz until his death on January 20, 1996.

programme.title: Ted Curson
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Avishai Cohen
programme.desc: Double bassist Avishai Cohen's trio with Noam David (drums) and Omri Mor (pianist) joined forces with the international Symphony Orchestra ‘INSO-Lviv', conducted by Christian Schumann. On June 24, 2017 they brought Avishai Cohen's signature blend of influences from Eastern Europe, American jazz and the Middle East to the Alfa Jazz Festival in Lviv, Ukraine. Cohen, who also supplies vocalizations in the Judeo-Spanish dialect Ladino, came to prominence internationally in the 1990s when jazz great Chick Corea offered him a place in his trio and a recording deal. Thirty years later, Cohen has become a house-hold name himself, leaving his Ukrainian audience enthralled and connected through the universal language of music.

programme.title: Stan Getz
programme.desc: Regarded as the greatest instrumental soloist of all-time, Stanley Gayetzky, famously known as Stan Getz emerged as one of the most significant musical forces in the world of jazz post World War II. With his distinctively warm and lyrical tone, Getz is fondly dubbed as ‘The Sound' because of his singularity and musical innovations. His commitment to music is evident from his long body of work that includes over 300 pieces of musical compositions. Ranked among America's top tenor saxophone players, Getz was a gifted saxophonist who could play just about anything on it, a quality that put him on top of the polls. He is accredited for playing some of the best jazz with some of the best jazzmen in the country. However, his personal life was a rollercoaster ride — tumultuous and loused up by abjection, alcohol, addiction and furious flare-ups. This program shows his last public performance, recorded at Munich Philharmonic Hall, Germany on July 18, 1990. Stan Getz (tenor sax) is accompanied by Kenny Barron (piano), Eddie Del Barrio and Frank Zottoli (synthesizers), Alex Blake (bass) and Terri Lyne Carrington (drums).

programme.title: Russian National Orchestra & Mikhail Pletnev
programme.desc: Russian conductor and pianist Mikhail Pletnev leads the Russian National Orchestra in performances of Nikolai Rimsky-Korsakov's music. On the program are Suite for Orchestra after the opera 'The Legend of the Invisible City of Kitezh and the Maiden Fevroniya', and Musical Pictures for Orchestra from the opera 'The Tale of Tsar Saltan'. Part of the latter is the famous 'Flight of the Bumblebee'. This concert was recorded at the Tchaikovsky Concert Hall of the Russian Philharmonic Society in 2017.

programme.title: Nikolay Lugansky
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Ballet of the Mariinsky Theatre
programme.desc: 'La Bayadère' is a three-act ballet by the French choreographer Marius Petipa. Set in 19th-century India, 'La Bayadère' tells the sad love story of the noble warrior Solor and his Nikia, a temple dancer or 'bayadère', who is poisoned by her rival, Gamzatti. The ballet features one of the most celebrated scenes of classical ballet, namely 'The Kingdom of the Shades'. In this iconic scene, Solor dreams, under the influence of opium, of reuniting with his beloved Nikia. This production of the ballet features the revised choreography by Vladimir Ponomarev and Vakhtang Chabukiani (1941), and includes dances by Konstantin Sergeyev and Nikolai Zubkovsky. The ballet's music, composed by Ludwig Minkus, is performed by the Mariinsky Orchestra under the direction of Boris Gruzin. Among the performers are Viktoria Tereshkina, Anastasia Matvienko, Vladimir Shklyarov, and Vladimir Ponomarev. This performance was recorded at the Mariinsky Theatre in St. Petersburg, Russia, in July 2014.

programme.title: Marina De Liso, Roberto Loreggian & Nicola Dal Maso
programme.sub-title: Vocal works by Monteverdi, Frescobaldi a. o.
programme.desc: The six-concert series “Io suono italiano” is dedicated to music of Italian composers and performed by Italian musicians on Italian instruments. The concert programs cover four centuries of music: from Girolamo Frescobaldi and Antonio Vivaldi to more contemporary music by Ennio Morricone and Azio Corghi. Each program presents more familiar pieces alongside lesser-known compositions. These performances were filmed at the atmospheric Teatro Sociale in Rovigo, Italy, in January 2021. In this program, Marina De Liso (soprano), Roberto Loreggian (harpsichord), and Nicola Dal Maso (violone) present vocal Baroque music. On the program are Benedetto Ferrari's Amanti io vi so dire, Barbara Strozzi's Lamento, Tradimento, and Che si può fare, Giovanni Felice Sances's Usurpator tiranno, Girolamo Frescobaldi's Partite sopra il Passacagli, and Se l'aura spira, Claudio Monteverdi's Ohimè ch'io cado, and Si dolce è il tormento.

programme.title: Conrad van Alphen
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Chen Xue-Hong, Zhang Cheng, Chen Yunjie & Chen Sa
programme.sub-title: Piano works by Chopin, Scriabin, Schumann & Liszt
programme.desc: In recent years, a new generation of Chinese pianists has emerged on the international scene. But there are many more young Chinese artists, who the Académie France-Chine aims to enable to deepen their art by introducing them to and giving them a platform within French and European culture. Four of these artists are Chen Xue-Hong, Zhang Cheng, Chen Yunjie and Chen Sa, who made their joint debut in Paris in this unique concert at the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées that showcased their extraordinary talent. On the program are Chopin's Piano Sonata No. 2, Schumann's Piano Sonata No. 1, Scriabin's Piano Sonata No. 8 and Paysage (No. 3) and Mazeppa (No. 4) from Liszt's Études d'Exécution Transcendante. The concert closes with Messiaen's Regard de l'Esprit de la joie (No. 10) from Vingt regards sur l'Enfant-Jésus.

programme.desc: Tracy, la última de tres amigas en tomar posesión de un antiguo velo de novia que compraron juntas, se ríe con cinismo de la leyenda de la prenda. Sin embargo, la magia también hará efecto en su vida.

programme.title: Bajo la luna de otoño
programme.desc: Mientras inspecciona un rancho para la compañía de expediciones de aventuras que la ha contratado, una mujer redescubre su pasión por la naturaleza, hasta entonces apagada, y se enamora del propietario del rancho.

programme.title: El Elegido

programme.title: The Son

programme.title: The Son
programme.desc: El joven Eli aprende a vivir entre los Comanches Eli y Pete discrepan sobre cómo responder a un ataque contra su casa.

programme.title: The Son
programme.desc: El joven Eli entrena como un guerrero, pero hace enemigos Eli y Phineas luchan por ayudar a la familia en Austin Pete se enfrenta a sus demonios.

programme.title: The Son
programme.desc: El joven Eli afronta en 1849 un reto que podría costarle la vida. En 1915, la familia McCullough elabora un arriesgado plan para plantar cara a sus enemigos.

programme.title: The Son
programme.desc: En 1849, un enemigo fuerza a Eli a cuestionarse su lealtad. En 1915, el pasado de los McCullough vuelve para atormentarlos con resultados mortales.

programme.title: The Son
programme.desc: El joven Eli debe tomar una decisión cuyas consecuencias entrañan un gran riesgo. Mientras tanto, los McCullough tratan de sobreponerse a la tragedia y Jeannie porfía en salvar a la familia.

programme.title: The Son
programme.desc: El joven Eli debe transitar con cuidado por el complicado mundo de las políticas tribales. Una crisis obliga a Peter y Sally a tomar una decisión que cambiará a la familia.

programme.title: The Son
programme.desc: Prairie Flower se siente dividida entre el amor y el deber. Mientras, la relación de Pete y Maria se vuelve más intensa y Elias involucra a Phineas en un secreto peligroso.

programme.desc: Max recupera la casa que compró para Eliza y, en ese mismo lugar, los dos le dan gracias a la vida por haber recibido otra oportunidad para estar juntos. Entretanto, la Interpol manipula los resultados de ADN de Alejandra.

programme.title: Al otro lado del muro
programme.desc: Paula suspende el monitoreo a Alejandra y brinda con Irving por la muerte de Sofía. Ernesto tiene dudas. Jennifer recibe la demanda de divorcio.

programme.title: Dueños del paraíso
programme.desc: En la selva colombiana, Anastasia y sus socios se encuentran con Lency, uno de sus proveedores. Elías y Renato llegan juntos a un bar, él intuye que lo quieren matar y trata de huir.

programme.title: Dueños del paraíso
programme.desc: Lency se molesta porque le quitan el transporte de la coca, le dice a Anastasia que Barragán está muerto y que sus cabezas ya tienen precio. Liberan a Analía, Renato no cree en su versión.

programme.title: Señora Acero
programme.desc: Sara le cuenta toda la verdad a su familia, pero Quintanilla no le cree. Sara y Briseida se enfrentan a muerte. La colombiana le dice al Gallo que no hará negocios con ella por burlarse.

programme.title: Señora Acero
programme.desc: Un extraño muy poderoso enamora a la Tuti a primera vista. Álvaro, hijo del Teca, acorrala a Sara y a Salvador a punta de pistola. Erick y Josefina recuperan el tiempo perdido.

programme.title: Señora Acero
programme.desc: Sara se encuentra con una escena impactante y no necesita explicaciones, pero Quintanilla sí. Álvaro se alía con la Tuti para llegar a Sara. Salvador sufre las consecuencias de su enfermedad.

programme.title: El Barón: La trampa
programme.desc: Es el cumpleaños de Sonny. La Madrina invita a Isabel a la celebración, si bien ella no sabe cuál es el plan que le tienen preparado. Isabel sirve de carnada.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera
programme.desc: Eva regresa a su trabajo de trailera para mantener a su nueva familia. Pablo la despide y le desea la mejor de las suertes en su nuevo camino. La visita de Cynthia a Soraya asombra a su hijo.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera
programme.desc: Soraya despierta del coma y recuerda que los quisieron matar. Cynthia teme que los descubran y junto a su esposo le exige a los Palacios que cumplan el trato. Bobby ahora se llama Luis Mogollón.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera
programme.desc: Soraya confunde a Bobby, quien se angustia porque ella cree que él es Pancho. Tampoco conoce a sus amigas, Marlene las vigila cuando comentan que perdió la memoria. Andy busca a Adriana.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera
programme.desc: Marlene se encarga de hacerle llegar a Eva la invitación para su boda con Armando. Él está arrepentido de haber perdido a su esposa. Evencio recuerda quién le dio la botella el día del crimen.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera
programme.desc: Se celebra por todo lo alto la boda del año entre Marlene y Armando. Adriana va a la fiesta con una intención, aunque Eva llega antes. El vigilante que buscaba Pablo desaparece sin dejar rastro.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera
programme.desc: Soraya intenta suicidarse al sufrir un ataque de pánico, sube hasta el último piso del hospital para lanzarse y acabar con su vida. Eva convence a Camila para que declare contra Nicolás Chávez.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera
programme.desc: Luis intenta hacerse respetar en la empresa. Robert se aprovecha de su ingenuidad y le dice cómo debe dirigir Panchopan, pero tanto el abogado como la directiva lo enfrentan en la reunión.

programme.title: Eva la Trailera

programme.title: Rebel Without A Kitchen

programme.title: Rebel Without A Kitchen

Matt learns how street food is done there and what it takes to succeed in the streets of the big apple.

programme.title: Rebel Without A Kitchen
programme.desc: The Philadelphia Night Market is the biggest street food festival in Philadelphia, and Matt's been invited to take part. In a place where sandwiches have been perfected over the past 80 years, Matt really wants to wow the masses since he's known as the sandwich guy back home. With his own Rebel take on the Philly cheese steak sandwich, he hopes he can impress the hard-core sandwich fans of Philadelphia.

programme.title: Mexico One Plate at a Time

programme.title: United Plates of America

programme.title: United Plates of America

programme.title: United Plates of America
programme.desc: Every body knows Los Angeles but not everyone has ventured into the Valley just over the Hollywood hills. Gary Takle is taking you into his home town to try the food in the San Fernando valley.

programme.title: Fearless In The Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Long Time Radio Host, First Time Cook
programme.desc: At 37, this party boy radio host of the morning show on The Edge is ready for change. Tired of eating "whatever I can find in the fridge nachos," Todd is looking for other ways to supplement his diet that does not include take-out. Todd loves to have people over for dinner but generally relies on catering from a local restaurant and a keg to keep his guests happy. Todd has recently purchased a new condo with a beautiful kitchen and would love to be able to make his own meals to wow his guests. Todd plays chicken in the kitchen when he attempts to make a chicken Caesar salad and then bones up on his beef knowledge at a restaurant where they do their own butchery. Todd then taps into his inner chef as he learns to make a pilsner chili and grilled cheese. Finally, it's boy meets grill when Todd prepares Philly cheese steaks for revelers at a large fundraising event. Christine will have to do her best if she hopes to unlock the necessary culinary skills to take this guy's guy from failure to fearless.

programme.title: Fearless In The Kitchen
programme.desc: Katherine is an independent 34-year-old marketing and advertising professional who has the confidence to get up and do large scale client presentations and sing the national anthem at professional baseball games, but she feels absolutely overwhelmed in the kitchen. When she isn't feasting on one of her boyfriend John's culinary creations, Katherine simply relies on jars of salsa, cheese strings and spoonfuls of peanut butter for sustenance. However, that doesn't mean she hasn't tried to cook in the past. Currently on her cooking “Claim to Shame List” are ‘Peanut Butter Salmon Cookies' -- she accidentally baked cookies on a cookie sheet that she had just previously cooked frozen fish on and did not clean properly. Mmmm fishy cookies. Katherine also lists her one and only disastrous attempt at a Thanksgiving dinner, in which she ended up trying to “shake” the giblets out of a semi-frozen turkey. To this day, Katherine does not understand how anyone can throw a dinner party without servants at their disposal. This is probably because she grew up in an Italian household where her extended family did all the work, and Katherine was relegated to setting the table. However, now that Katherine is in a serious relationship with a man who is able to cook, she feels this is time for her to change her lifestyle and become more proactive in the kitchen. Christine will have her work cut out for her as she tries to take this non-cook from “inept” to “enthralled” in the kitchen.

programme.title: The Migrant Kitchen
programme.desc: In American restaurants, immigrants are the backbone of the kitchen. Stories include Guatemalan brothers who bring indispensable knowledge and skill to two Los Angeles institutions, Providence and Connie and Ted's. Meanwhile, the Filipino food movement finds chefs of various backgrounds and professional levels embracing tradition, forming community and pushing forward the cuisine of their heritage together. (S1 E01)

programme.desc: Nigella shares gorgeous recipes, including a fluffy white loaf, a fried chicken sandwich, cheesecake ice cream, fear-free fish stew, and a simple yet daring cherry flambé that uses frozen cherries and kirsch.

programme.title: Nigella Lawson's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella shows her take on the bhorta, to which she introduces a very British but very familiar ingredient. Next, she introduces slow cooked lamb shanks with wide noodles and cabbage. Then, ripe bananas are the key ingredient in two desserts.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke continues his street food journey in Bangkok where he searches for the best Tom Yum soup and entertains the locals with a rare Thai delicacy.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys: A Colorful Portugal by Rail
programme.desc: In Portugal, in the magnificent Douro River Valley, a steam locomotive and a small historic train will take you in turn to the water's edge, on the only railroad line in the world that crosses three sites.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.sub-title: Secret Family Recipies in Ho Chi Minh
programme.desc: Luke searches to find Ho Chi Minh's old markets before they disappear due to District 1's rapid development. Join Luke as he gathers fresh ingredients from a local market, tries some traditional delicacies, and follows his sweet tooth to Marou.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke's next stop is the destination of Dalat. Follow Luke as he visits the Art Deco Dalat Train Station, experiences the French influence of the region with a Father and Son chef duo, and discovers why Dalat is known as the'Land Of Eternal Spring!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.sub-title: Exploring The 'Wet Market' Of Nha Trang
programme.desc: Luke hops on the train in Ga Thap Cham, and finds himself in Nha Trang- a region of Vietnam with a similar vibe to the French Riviera! Luke hand-catches seafood, looks for some fresh durian fruit, and visits a barramundi farm to harvest sea grapes!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.sub-title: Fiery Chili Sauce in Hoi An
programme.desc: Luke is in the town of Hoi An. He visits a family who has been making chilli sauce for 5 generations, stops by the Historic Japanese Covered Bridge built in 1719, and meets a 4th generation blacksmith who is creating knives from bomb shells.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.sub-title: Seafood With A Bang, Here In Da Nang
programme.desc: On this stop of his Vietnam journey, Luke visits the bustling seaside city of Da Nang. Join him as he visits the spectacular Dragon Bridge, looks up some succulent shimp on the beach, and stumbles upon a tiny fishing village packed with restaurants.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke visits Huê- famous for its heritage and cuisine and Con Hen Village to learn a technique for de-shelling baby clams, searches for best bowl of spicy beef noodle soup, and stops by a business where they they make turmeric vermicelli noodle salad!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.sub-title: Tasty Ant Eggs in Ninh Binh
programme.desc: After a long trip from Hue, Luke finally gets some rest in the scenic Ninh Bihn. Tag along as Luke meets with a local rice farmer, takes a canoe out for some crab hunting, and ventures deep into the jungle in search of a local delicacy- red ant eggs!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.sub-title: Searching For The Banh Da Noodle in Hai Phong
programme.desc: Luke is in northern Vietnam- Hai Phong! This is the birthplace of his partner Lynne. Join Luke as he meets some of Lynne's family for the first time, and tries the delcious Banh Da Noodle- a tasty dish that Hai Phong is renowned for!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke's penultimate stop on his train journey is Hanoi. Follow Luke on his hunt for an iconic Hanoi dish, Bun Cha. Luke takes a ride on the Reunification Express railway, and visits the famous Hoan Kiem Lake for some delcious magenta sticky rice.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Nearing the end of his Vitenamese journey, Luke stops in Sapa, a hilltop region that he remembers from his travels fifteen years ago. Join Luke as he rekindles a friendship from his previous visit and learns about a local dish of dried buffalo.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Street Food Asia
programme.desc: Luke arrives in Saigon for a breakfast of iced coffee with condensed milk and a vermicelli noodle dish. Later, he visits his favourite Pho restaurant, then cooks up some spicy Chilli Crab Claws.

programme.title: Race Across the World S04 E05
programme.desc: The bafta-winning hit series ‘Race Across the World' is well underway. After journeying through Japan, South Korea, Vietnam and Cambodia and crossing the border into Thailand, the four remaining teams have reached the halfway mark in the race across Asia to the Indonesian paradise of Lombok. The leader board has been flipped on its head, and for the first time, in a dramatic turnaround retired couple Stephen and Viv are at the top and Alfie and Owen have dropped to the bottom with an 11-hour gap separating them.

programme.title: Race Across the World S04 E09

Together, they look back at the 51-day thrilling adventure that began in northernmost Japan and covered 15,000 kilometres through seven countries, taking in some of the world's most beautiful, populous and remote places to reach the finish line. Unseen moments will be shown, and shocking revelations made.

programme.title: Angry Swarms
programme.desc: Matt, Lou, and Lyndah try to repo a car from a man who is not afraid to throw some punches. Then, Matt and Lou face a dilemma when they realize their target vehicle is filled with angry bees. And, Sonia and Froy hit the airwaves to reclaim a truck that's being used in a radio broadcast stunt.

programme.title: Repo Outside the Box
programme.desc: First, Matt and Sonia attempt to repossess a car from a group of cleaver-wielding short-order cooks with a taste for violence. Then, it's trouble in paradise as Matt and Froy take on the task of reclaiming a beach-scene-on-wheels filled with irate models. And finally, Sonia and Matt find themselves outmatched by a well-armed street gang and its charismatic leader.

programme.title: Ruffians and Wrestlers
programme.desc: Sonia and Matt have an unpleasant confrontation with a group of skateboarding ruffians who don't want to give up their car. Froy and Sonia try to take a vehicle from the world's most volatile couple. Plus, Lou and Froy wind up in unknown territory when they take an order to repossess from a squad of wannabe backyard wrestlers.

programme.title: Up Up and Away
programme.desc: First, it's up, up, and away, for Lou, Froy, and Matt. All of their skills are tested when they attempt to take down not one, but two hot air balloons. Then, Lou and Froy find themselves in the wrong part of town, repo'ing a sports car from a former gang member who's not afraid to go back to jail. Finally, Lou and Lyndah break in Frankie, the new guy, with a repo that turns into a confrontation with three spray-paint-packing graffiti girls.

programme.title: Don't Cry, Bro
programme.desc: First, Matt and Froy track a car to the compound of what seems to be a polygamist cult. Will their charismatic leader put up a fight for his Prius, or will he have his followers do it for him?

programme.title: Bullseye!
programme.desc: Sonia, the new boss, is still struggling to keep the team in line, but when tragedy strikes on a routine repossession, her reign at the top faces its biggest challenge yet. Matt and Froy hit the club to take a sports car away from a deadbeat DJ.

programme.title: Adapt, Improvise, and Overcome
programme.desc: Lou plans an elaborate sting operation to infiltrate a cult, but everything goes awry when the new guys aren't prepared and Matt is left picking up the pieces.

programme.title: Warzone at the Worksite
programme.desc: Ronnie's gotten a raise and a promotion, and Carlos's jealousy sends him looking for more appreciation. But when his quest for glory goes wrong, will it turn the entire team against him? Matt and Sonia wrangle with a small business owner with some violent cargo in the back of his truck, Sonia and Froy have some hard lessons for a bratty hipster, and Froy and Carlos get in too deep with a dockworker who will destroy anything and anyone who messes with his car.

programme.title: First Class Clown
programme.desc: Matt and Froy are sent on a wild goose chase around L.A for a mini-van, and when they find their debtor selling contraband out of the trunk Matt is further convinced that Lyndah has been scheming against him.

programme.title: Get That Prowler
programme.desc: First, Lou, Sonia, and Froy unfurl an elaborate sting when they repo a taxicab by tricking its driver into picking them up. But what to do with his ferociously inebriated client? Then, Matt and Froy attempt to repo a DeLorean from a debtor whose car isn't all that's stuck in the 80's. Finally, Lou and Sonia square off against an overgrown child who isn't giving up his Plymouth Prowler without a tussle.

programme.title: It Ain't No Joke!
programme.desc: In this kick-off to the final season, the cast takes viewers back through the most outrageous, comical, and memorable repos of the series. And, get a sneak peek of the final season of Operation Repo.

programme.title: The Waiting Game
programme.desc: While Froy waits on news from his lawyer about his warrant in Texas, he considers taking matters into his own hands. Lou and Froy bust some slick moves in an attempt to wrangle a car from a debtor who is prepared to battle. Then, when one woman behaves like a child, Matt puts her in timeout, but worries he may have gone too far. And finally, Lou, Matt, and Froy take on an jealous husband with deadly aim.

programme.title: Doggin' and Draggin'
programme.desc: When Carlos plays a repossession prank that almost lands Matt and Froy in jail, Lou tasks Froy with burying the hatchet.

programme.title: Who Wants Ice Cream?
programme.desc: First, Sonia, Matt, Froy, and Lou roll up and roll on a gang of brothers to gain possession of an Infinity. Next, Matt and Froy stand in the way of a drugged debtor keeping his car. programme.title: Angry Planet | Under Pressure
programme.desc: It's dark, it's cold, there is no air to breath and everything is under extreme pressure. George begins his exploration of the deep with a well-preserved shipwreck in the st.lawrence River, then heads west to dive with undersea explorers Chris Harvey-Clark and Phil Nuyyton, and investigate the newly discovered “Black Smoker” deepsea vents. The show climaxes in Curacao, where we dive to 1000 feet aboard Nuyyton's newest submersible.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Italia Wild
programme.desc: Italy is one of the very few nations in the world that regularly gets three major extreme natural phenomena – volcanos, flooding, and avalanches. Eastern flanks of the volcano to slip by up to two metres, and many houses on the flanks of the volcano experienced structural damage. The eruption also completely destroyed the Rifugio Sapienza, on the southern flank of the volcano. The Rifugio was the site of a cable car station which had previously been destroyed in the 1983 eruption both have now been rebuilt. The most recent eruption was in November 2006.

programme.title: Angry Planet | By Camel to Timbuktu
programme.desc: It is time for “Angry Planet” to do a serious desert episode. What better place to do it than this fabled city in the middle of the mighty Sahara Desert.This almost mythical place really does exist. And it really is still possible to travel there by camel caravan, riding the infamous “Ships of the Desert”. Extreme heat, dehydration, possible sand storms, frigid night temperatures and desert predators are our potential companions for the trip.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Java
programme.desc: The most notorious volcanic explosion in history came from the small island of Krakatoa, off the coast of Java, The tiny island was pulverized and the giant explosion was felt half way around the world. Java is at the heart of the Ring of Fire, and so has seen many volcanoes, mud slides and earthquakes through its long history.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Elephant Cave
programme.desc: Possibly the home of the deadly Marberg Ebola virus, Kitum Cave is high on Mt.Elgon straddling the Kenya-Uganda border. The deep, waterfall-shrouded cave has been carved out over millennia by elephants in search of salt. When host george kourounis gets bitten by one of the bats&mldr well, you'll have to see the show to find out what happens.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Costa Rica
programme.desc: Costa Rica is a tiny country jammed with diversity and adventure. George explores the volcanoes of Costa Rica with an international team of scientists, rafts down whitewater rivers in full flood, canyoneers down raging waterfalls, and handles some of deadliest snakes this tropical country has to offer.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Arctic Winter
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore the frigid Canadian arctic in the depths of winter. The team first uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head north through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet | Highway to Hail
programme.desc: George and three side-kicks head straight into the “Bearcage”, looking to get beat up by the biggest, baddest hailstones that Texas and Oklahoma can throw at them. They find it – and their stormchasing vehicles don't look too pretty after coming through these massive storms.

programme.desc: In celebration of Marie Claire's 20th anniversary, the designers are asked to make a look that could be worn twenty years in the future in the magazine. The catch? They have to look to the past…20 years to be exact, for inspiration.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: After a field trip to Red Robin, the designers are challenged to take an all-American staple, a men's suit, and transform it into a high fashion women's look. The suits they are given are from all different fashion eras, and the winner of the last challenge is asked to choose for the rest of the competitors.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: Heidi asks the designers to create a red carpet look that she could wear to the Creative Arts Emmys. When Heidi is less than pleased in the workroom, she tells most of the designers to start over. On the Runway, two designers are eliminated.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: For the second Team Challenge of the season, the designers work in pairs to create looks that could be worn for a “nontraditional wedding.” They must design one look for the ceremony, and one for the reception. Dita Von Teese introduces the challenge and serves as guest judge.

programme.title: Project Runway
programme.desc: With the help of Chopard, the designers are asked to create an extravagant eveningwear piece inspired by jewelry of their choosing. They also draw inspiration from the Charles James exhibit at the MET, and Tim decides to use his “Tim Gunn Save” on one designer.
